description_long: This object is a vintage desktop computer setup, featuring a large CRT monitor with a brown accent panel on top, a full-sized keyboard with numerous black keys, and a separate external device connected by cables, likely a cassette data recorder. The monitor has a convex glass screen and a rectangular label on the front, with a power button and indicator light. The keyboard is integrated into a wide, rectangular base with a metallic finish and includes a numeric keypad, function keys, and various switches. The external device is boxy, black, and has a slot on top, with visible wiring connecting it to the main unit. The entire setup is reminiscent of early personal computers from the late 1970s or early 1980s, such as the Tandy/Radio Shack TRS-80.
description: A vintage desktop computer setup with a large CRT monitor, full-sized keyboard, and an external cassette data recorder, all connected by visible cables. The design features a metallic and black color scheme with brown accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT monitor, full keyboard with numeric keypad, and the external cassette recorder to the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the monitor, the keyboard's profile, and the cassette recorder's wiring.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel of the monitor with ventilation, handle, and manufacturer labels, as well as the rear of the cassette recorder.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the monitor's sloped back, the keyboard's angled profile, and the cassette recorder's position.
